meaning: adj.不知名的,没看惯的,陌生的,不可思议的,奇怪的adv.非常
examples: But experience suggests that an apparently co-operative Iran can be trickier to cope with than an obviously unco-operative one.
Fears that unco-ordinated financial regulation could undermine the fragile economic recovery sparked a crisis of confidence among investors.
TIM GEITHNER has reportedly grown so exasperated with unco-operative regulators that he recently blasted them with an expletive-filled rant.
But her critics say the result will be a disparate, unco-ordinated system - not greater fairness.
He refrained from mentioning the fact that the king can dissolve an unco-operative parliament, and veto unhelpful bills.
